steps.title: Map the passage's three-part structure
steps.content: The text does three things in order: (1) presents a finding about Arizona pocket mice — moonlight inhibits their activity — and explains why (predation risk outweighs foraging gain); (2) notes that most other nocturnal mammals behave similarly; (3) introduces Azara's night monkeys as an exception and explains their opposite pattern (visual foraging shifts the risk-reward balance). Every part of the passage includes an explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each description against that structure:
  • "It introduces an observation of a behavioral pattern, presents an explanation for the pattern, and then describes an exception to the pattern that casts doubt on that explanation.": The night monkey exception does not cast doubt on the cost-benefit explanation — it is itself explained by the same framework. ✗
  • "It describes and accounts for a finding, characterizes the finding as representative of a general pattern, and then describes and accounts for an exception to that pattern.": Matches all three stages exactly: finding + explanation, general pattern, exception + explanation. ✓
  • "It discusses two different responses to the same natural phenomenon, explains why one of those responses occurs, and then suggests that the other response still needs to be explained.": Both responses are explained; nothing is left unexplained. ✗
  • "It presents and explains a study result, indicates that the result is similar to the results of many other studies, and then attributes a conflicting study result to a difference in that study’s methods.": The night monkey difference is attributed to biology (visual foraging), not to methodological differences. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
